solutions.solution: Statements: a. Some Indians are not Africans. b. All Africans are Asians. c. Some Asians are Americans. Conclusions: I. Some Indians are not Asians. II. All Africans are Americans. IV. Some Americans are not Africans. 1) Only I follows 2) Only II follows 3) Only III follows 4) Only I and IV follows 5) Either II or IV follows

Statements: a. Some cards are papers. b. All papers are vans. c. Some vans are trams. Conclusions: I. Some cards are trams. II. Some trams are vans. III. Some trams are papers. IV. Some vans are cards. 1) I and III follow 2) I and IV follow 3) Only IV follows 4) II, III and IV follow 5) I, II and III follow

Statements: a. No fruit is a mango. b. No oranges is a potato. c. All mangoes are oranges. Conclusions: I. Some fruits are not oranges. II. Some oranges are not fruits. III. No mango is a potato. IV. Some oranges are fruits. 1) Only III and I follow 2) Only II and IV follow 3) Either II or IV follow 4) Only II and III follow 5) Either II or IV and III follow

Statements: a. Some birds are docks. b. All cats are clocks. c. No frock is a clock. Conclusions: I. All cats are clocks. II. No frock is a clock. III. Some docks are not frocks. IV. Some docks are frocks. 1) Either III or IV follows 2) Only I and II follow 3) I, II and III follow 4) I, II and either III or IV follow 5) I and either III or IV follow

Statements: a. Some tablets are capsules. b. No capsule is a medicine. c. All medicines are syrups. Conclusions: I. Some tablets are not medicines. II. Some medicines are not capsules. III. Some syrups are medicines. IV. Some syrups are not capsules. 1) I and III follow 2) I, III and IV follow 3) IV and IV follow 4) II and IV follow 5) All follow

Statements: a. All coats are pants. b. No pant is a shirt. c. Some shirts are vests. Conclusions: I. Some vests are shirts. II. Some coats are shirts. III. No coat is a shirt. IV. Some vests are not coats. 1) I and III follow 2) I and IV follow 3) I and III follow 4) I, III and IV follow 5) I, IV and either II or III follow

Statements: a. All chalks are cheese. b. No cheese is a ship. c. Some herds are ships. Conclusions: I. Some herds are not chalks. II. Some herds are not cheese. III. Some cheese are not herds. IV. No chalk is a ship. 1) III and IV follow 2) II, III and IV follow 3) II and IV follow 4) III and II follow 5) I and IV follow

Statements: a. Some birds are peacocks. b. No cock is a hen. c. All hens are peacocks. Conclusions: I. Some cocks are peacocks. II. Some birds are hens. III. No cock is a peacock. IV. Some peacocks are not cocks. 1) Either I or IV follows 2) II and IV follow 3) Either I or III and IV follow 4) Either I or III, II and IV follow 5) Only II follows
